document: South Africa National Cybersecurity Policy Framework (NCPF) 2015

sections.heading: 1 . Introduction
sections.title: Introduction to South African Cybersecurity Policy Framework
sections.summary: This section introduces the National Cybersecurity Policy Framework (NCPF) as part of South Africa's Justice, Crime Prevention and Security Cluster. It outlines the strategic importance of cybersecurity in the context of national security, economic growth, and digital integration. The framework aims to address cyber threats such as cybercrime, cyber terrorism, and cyber espionage while balancing the benefits of information technology with security risks.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes the foundational context for cybersecurity policy in South Africa, emphasizing the need for coordinated national responses to cyber threats. It does not directly specify transfer mechanisms but sets the stage for future regulatory requirements.

requirements.text: The NCPF sets out a National Cybersecurity Policy Framework for South Africa
The framework addresses cybersecurity threats including cybercrime, cyber terrorism, and cyber espionage
The framework promotes a cybersecurity culture and compliance with minimum security standards
The framework strengthens intelligence collection, investigation, prosecution and judicial processes for cybercrime
The framework establishes public-private partnerships for national and international action plans
The framework ensures protection of National Critical Information Infrastructure
The framework promotes a comprehensive legal framework governing cyberspace

sections.source_quote: The NCPF Policy Framework therefore recognises that Cybersecurity threats and the combating thereof have a personal, national and international context.
sections.relevance_reason: Core policy on cybersecurity and critical infrastructure

sections.heading: have the potential to impact on our national security and economy.
sections.title: South African Cybersecurity Context and Challenges
sections.summary: This section details South Africa's cybersecurity challenges, including the increasing cybercrime threats, the lack of coordinated legislation, and the vulnerability of critical information infrastructure. It highlights the need for a unified approach to cybersecurity that involves government, the private sector, and civil society to address cyber threats and protect national security.
sections.dtia_summary: This section identifies South Africa's vulnerability to cyber threats and the need for coordinated national responses, which may impact data transfer and processing activities across borders.

requirements.text: South Africa faces significant cybercrime threats targeting financial institutions
The absence of coordinated cybersecurity legislation creates vulnerabilities
South Africa needs mechanisms for proactive and coordinated national response to cyber threats
Cybersecurity is everyone's responsibility, including public and private sectors

sections.source_quote: The banking sector is especially vulnerable to cybercrime.
sections.relevance_reason: Highlights context for data security but not direct compliance rules

sections.heading: freedom of expression.
sections.title: Purpose of the National Cybersecurity Policy Framework
sections.summary: This section outlines the purpose of the NCPF to create a secure, dependable, and trustworthy cyber environment that protects critical information infrastructure while strengthening shared human values and understanding of cybersecurity. It emphasizes the need for collaboration between government, business, and civil society to enhance national security and economic well-being.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines the purpose of the NCPF, which includes protecting critical information infrastructure and ensuring cybersecurity, but does not specify data transfer mechanisms.

requirements.text: The NCPF aims to create a secure, dependable, reliable and trustworthy cyber environment
The framework ensures protection of critical information infrastructure
The framework strengthens shared human values and understanding of cybersecurity

sections.source_quote: The purpose of the NCPF is to create a secure, dependable, reliable and trustworthy cyber environment that facilitates the protection of critical information infrastructure whilst strengthening shared human values and understanding of Cybersecurity in support of national security imperatives and the economy.
sections.relevance_reason: Core purpose for data protection and cross-border transfers

sections.heading: Key Objectives of the NCPF
sections.title: Key Objectives of the National Cybersecurity Policy Framework
sections.summary: This section lists the strategic priorities of the NCPF, including centralizing cybersecurity coordination, fostering cooperation between government, the private sector, and civil society, promoting international cooperation, developing cybersecurity skills, promoting a cybersecurity culture, and ensuring compliance with technical and operational standards.
sections.dtia_summary: This section outlines the strategic objectives for cybersecurity in South Africa, which may influence data transfer and processing activities through the emphasis on critical infrastructure protection and international cooperation.

requirements.text: Centralize coordination of cybersecurity activities through relevant structures and strategies
Foster cooperation and coordination between government, private sector, and civil society
Promote international cooperation in cybersecurity
Develop requisite skills, research and development capacity in cybersecurity
Promote a culture of cybersecurity
Promote compliance with appropriate technical and operational cybersecurity standards

sections.source_quote: The NCPF articulates the overall aim and objectives of the South African Government and sets out strategic priorities that will be pursued to achieve these objectives.

sections.heading: Capacity to Respond to Cybersecurity Imperatives

sections.title: Public-private cybersecurity coordination through Cybersecurity Hub
sections.summary: This section establishes a Cybersecurity Hub within the Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services to coordinate and consult between public sector, private sector, and civil society on cybersecurity matters. The Hub operates under national security guidelines from the JCPS Cybersecurity Response Committee and has specific responsibilities including stakeholder identification, sector CSIRT coordination, information dissemination, best practice guidance, awareness campaigns, and promoting compliance with cybersecurity standards. The Hub also facilitates the development of additional sector-specific CSIRTs that coordinate incident response within their sectors.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes a coordination mechanism for cybersecurity stakeholders but does not directly address data transfer mechanisms. It may indirectly influence data handling practices through sector-specific security requirements but has no explicit data transfer implications for DTIAs.
sections.authority: Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services

requirements.text: Establish Cybersecurity Hub within Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services
Operate Cybersecurity Hub under national security guidelines issued by JCPS Cybersecurity Response Committee
Coordinate cybersecurity activities with JCPS CRC and identify stakeholders
Develop public-private relationships and collaborate with sector CSIRTs
Disseminate cybersecurity information to sector CSIRTs, vendors, and technology experts
Provide best practice guidance on ICT security for government, business, and civil society
Initiate cybersecurity awareness campaigns
Promote compliance with JCPS Cybersecurity Response Committee standards
Encourage development of additional sector CSIRTs
Sector CSIRTs must act as point of contact for cybersecurity matters
Coordinate cybersecurity incident response within sector
Facilitate information and technology sharing within sector
Facilitate information sharing and technology exchange with other sector CSIRTs
Establish sector-specific national security standards with JCPS Cybersecurity Response Committee
Develop agreed measures for sector cybersecurity

sections.source_quote: The Cybersecurity Hub will be operated within the DOC in accordance with national security guidelines and standards issued by the JCPS Cybersecurity Response Committee.

sections.heading: Verification of Information Security Products and Systems
sections.title: National Information Security Verification Framework
sections.summary: This section promotes the development of a National Information Security Verification Framework to independently assess and certify products and systems that process or store information impacting national security. The framework requires facilitating partnerships between South Africa and countries with assessment capacity, government-private sector-academic institutions, establishing national regulations for verification, coordinating government institutions for implementation, and creating a central body for verification functions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes requirements for verifying information security products and systems that could impact data transfers involving national security, but does not directly address cross-border data transfer mechanisms for DTIAs.

requirements.text: Develop National Information Security Verification Framework
Facilitate partnerships between South Africa and countries with information security assessment capacity
Facilitate partnerships between government, private sector, and academic institutions for information security assessments
Develop national regulations for verification of information security products and systems
Facilitate partnerships among government institutions for implementation of verification
Establish body to centrally coordinate national verification functions

sections.source_quote: The NCPF therefore promotes the facilitation by the JCPS Cybersecurity Response Committee and the National Cybersecurity Hub of the development of a National Information Security Verification Framework

sections.heading: NCII Protection
sections.title: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection
sections.summary: This section addresses the identification and protection of South Africa's critical information infrastructure (NCII) which is often privately owned. It promotes the development of an NCII Strategy that includes regulations for information classification, third-party access, authentication, storage of critical databases, incident management, business continuity, and physical/technical protection of NCII. The strategy requires collaboration between the private sector, SOEs, and government agencies to implement NCII protection.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes specific requirements for protecting critical information infrastructure, which may impact data storage and handling practices for NCII but does not directly address cross-border data transfers for DTIAs.

requirements.text: Identify all National Critical Information Infrastructure (NCII)
Develop National Critical Information Infrastructure regulations for information classification
Develop National Critical Information Infrastructure regulations for third-party access to NCII
Develop National Critical Information Infrastructure regulations for access and authentication on NCII
Develop National Critical Information Infrastructure regulations for storage and archiving of critical databases
Develop National Critical Information Infrastructure regulations for incident management and business continuity
Develop National Critical Information Infrastructure regulations for physical and technical protection of NCII

sections.source_quote: The NCPF therefore seeks to ensure that appropriate steps are taken to ascertain that all National Critical Information Infrastructure (NCII) are identified and properly protected from a variety of threats.
sections.relevance_reason: Critical infrastructure protection directly impacts data security and breach handling

sections.heading: Cryptography
sections.title: Cryptography Regulation
sections.summary: This section recognizes the need for regulating cryptography given its critical role in secure communications. It requires reviewing existing legislation and regulations and developing an integrated regulatory framework for cryptography to ensure secure communications across the country.
sections.dtia_summary: This section addresses cryptographic standards which could impact data security but does not directly address cross-border data transfer mechanisms for DTIAs.

requirements.text: Review existing legislation and regulations on cryptography
Develop an integrated regulatory framework for cryptography

sections.source_quote: The NCPF therefore provides for the regulation of cryptography given the critical role it plays in ensuring improved secure communications.
sections.relevance_reason: Cryptography regulation critical for secure communications and financial data

sections.title: National E-identity and PKI Strategy
sections.summary: This section promotes the development of an integrated National E-identity and Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) strategy to address authentication, confidentiality, integrity, non-repudiation, and regulatory frameworks for electronic identity. The strategy aims to support e-government services and secure online transactions while ensuring security, confidentiality, and integrity of information.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes requirements for electronic identity and PKI which could impact data sharing practices but does not directly address cross-border data transfer mechanisms for DTIAs.
sections.authority: Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services

requirements.text: Develop integrated National E-identity and PKI strategy
Address authentication and securing identities of parties in e-transactions
Ensure confidentiality of information
Ensure integrity of information or processes
Ensure non-repudiation of transactions
Establish structure and regulatory framework for E-identity and PKI

sections.source_quote: The NCPF seeks to address the fragmented approach by promoting the development of an integrated National E-identity and PKI strategy.
sections.relevance_reason: Core data protection and security strategy

sections.heading: 1 1 . Promote and Strengthen Local and International Cooperation
sections.title: Public-Private-Civil Sector Cooperation
sections.summary: This section promotes public-private-civil sector collaboration for cybersecurity through joint knowledge sharing, trusted forums, threat vulnerability understanding, and industry perspectives. It emphasizes the need for local cooperation and international collaboration to combat cybercrime, including participation in regional and international cybersecurity forums, forging bilateral and multilateral partnerships, and affiliating with relevant international organizations.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes cooperation mechanisms that could indirectly influence data transfer practices but does not directly address cross-border data transfer mechanisms for DTIAs.

requirements.text: Promote public-private-civil sector collaboration for cybersecurity
Establish trusted forums for cybersecurity knowledge sharing
Develop understanding of cybersecurity threats and vulnerabilities
Engage industry perspectives for cybersecurity
Participate in regional and international cybersecurity forums
Forge bilateral and multilateral partnerships for cybersecurity
Affiliate with relevant international organizations for cybersecurity

sections.source_quote: The NCPF thus promotes that a Cyber Defence Strategy, that is informed by the National Security Strategy of South Africa, be developed, guided by the JCPS Cybersecurity Response Committee.

sections.heading: 1 1 . Promote and Strengthen Local and International Cooperation (cont.)
sections.title: Cybersecurity responsibilities of government departments and private sector
sections.summary: This section outlines specific cybersecurity responsibilities for South African government departments including the Department of Police and SAPS for cybercrime prevention, the Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services for ICT policies and the National Cybersecurity Advisory Council, the Department of Defence for cyber defense, and the Department of Science and Technology for cybersecurity research. It also mandates private sector entities to implement information security measures equivalent to government standards and requires all state organs to align their ICT practices with the National Cybersecurity Policy Framework.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes government responsibilities for cybersecurity coordination and mandates private sector information security equivalence, which impacts cross-border data handling through national security frameworks and international cooperation requirements.
sections.authority: South African Department of Police and SAPS

requirements.text: SSA shall develop and implement regulations for cybersecurity
SSA shall collect intelligence locally and internationally
SSA shall conduct cybersecurity investigations and report on South Africa's cybersecurity situation
Department of Police and SAPS shall prevent, investigate and combat cybercrime
Department of Police and SAPS shall develop cybercrime policies and strategies
Department of Police and SAPS shall provide specialized investigative capacity
Department of Police and SAPS shall interact with national and international stakeholders
Department of Police and SAPS shall develop operational priorities for fighting child sexual/physical abuse material online
Department of Police and SAPS shall address massive attacks against information systems like denial-of-service attacks
Department of Police and SAPS shall combat identity fraud
Department of Police and SAPS shall develop cross-border law enforcement cooperation
Department of Police and SAPS shall promote public-private cooperation to fight cybercrime
Department of Police and SAPS shall participate in international initiatives like UN High Level Expert Group on Cybersecurity
Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services shall develop and implement ICT policies and regulations
Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services shall assist in strategic direction for cybersecurity
Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services shall build trust and confidence in secure ICT use
Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services shall advise Minister on cybersecurity policy and technical issues
Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services shall establish the National Cybersecurity Advisory Council
Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services shall establish the Cybersecurity Hub
Department of Defence and Military Veterans shall coordinate, account for and implement cyber defense measures
Department of Science and Technology shall develop national capacity development programs
Department of Science and Technology shall facilitate national cybersecurity R&D agenda
All other state organs must align ICT policies and practices with NCPF for cybersecurity
Private sector must implement information security measures equivalent to government standards
NCPF promotes cooperation between private sector security bodies and government bodies
Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services and National Cybersecurity Hub will facilitate private sector cooperation
Each person must ensure updated malware protection on their ICT infrastructure
Each person must report information security incidents to police or CSIRT
Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services will facilitate awareness campaigns for cybersecurity

sections.source_quote: The implementation of these responsibilities by SSA shall include aspects of developing and implementing regulations, collecting intelligence both locally and internationally, conducting necessary Cybersecurity investigations and reporting on South Africa's Cybersecurity situation.
sections.relevance_reason: Government cybersecurity mandates for private sector alignment
